title: ADR-005 fix never implemented — confirm handoff routes to Dev Clone this time
description: ADR-005 (token credit() negative-balance floor) was scoped on 2026-06-01 but the change never landed (both Store drivers still write the unclamped balance, confirmed 2026-06-02). This repeats the ADR-001 pattern where the architect's architecture.analysis.completed event did not result in an engineer implementation. I have re-issued ADR-005 as a self-sufficient 1-task/3pt handoff. Confirm the chain trigger actually delivers this to Dev Clone (implement → PR → QA), rather than to Code Review on an empty changeset.

title: PR #1 (ADR-005 credit() floor) is red — needs migration landed on main before it can merge
description: The ADR-005 token credit() negative-balance floor is correctly implemented on branch dev-clone/credit-balance-floor and PR #1 is open vs main, but the increment fails all 5 regression tests (pglitePath is not a function) because the branch was cut from main while the firestore/pglite persistence migration it depends on is still UNMERGED (uncommitted on dev-clone/charge-error-detection). The fix cannot be QA-merged in isolation, and dragging the whole migration into this 3-point PR would violate the small-focused-PR / scope rules. A human routing decision is required.

title: PR #1 (ADR-005 credit() floor) — Code Review verdict REQUEST_CHANGES: do not merge until persistence migration lands on main
description: Independent code review confirms PR #1's clamp logic is correct but the increment cannot build or pass its own tests against main: pglite-store.ts:11 imports pglitePath (branch config.ts exports sqlitePath); firestore-store.ts:17-18 import @/lib/firestore/admin and COLLECTION_PREFIX (absent on branch); store.ts:80-83 dispatch on firestore/pglite which the branch dbDriver() never returns. Root cause: the firestore/pglite persistence migration is unmerged (uncommitted on dev-clone/charge-error-detection). A human routing decision is required — do not QA-merge a red PR, and do not balloon this 3-point PR with the whole migration.

title: PHI egress to external observability (LightTrack + Langfuse Cloud) — unaddressed across 4 scans, needs HIPAA/BAA decision
description: Two HIGH findings send full unredacted LLM prompts containing PHI (patient name, account number, provider, bill charges) to external observability sinks with no redaction and no BAA/consent gate. They have now survived 4 consecutive security scans without remediation. This is a genuine business/compliance decision (redact at the choke point vs. gate on a signed BAA vs. accept risk), not an operational issue — routing to a human.
